perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Initial Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. This agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one-year periods (each a “Renewal Term”), unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the Initial Term or then-current Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Term' to 'Initial Term' and adding an automatic renewal clause, we create a contradiction. Section 1.01 originally defines the Term as three years. The added text introduces the possibility of renewal, making the actual term potentially longer. This contradicts the initial definition and creates uncertainty regarding the contract's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Initial Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. This agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one-year periods (each a “Renewal Term”), unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the Initial Term or then-current Renewal Term. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the term of the agreement as three years ('Term'). The changed text redefines 'Term' as 'Initial Term' and introduces an automatic renewal clause, extending the agreement beyond the initial three years under certain circumstances. This creates a contradiction as the 'Term' is no longer strictly three years, as defined in the original text and referenced elsewhere in the document, fitting the definition of an 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: PrimeCall shall negotiate and contract on behalf of DeltaThree to out-source customer service for users of the Calling Card. DeltaThree shall reimburse PrimeCall for all customer service expenses directly related to users of the Calling Card. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the level of customer service shall be subject to the approval of DeltaThree.
perturbation.changed_text: PrimeCall shall negotiate and contract on behalf of DeltaThree to out-source customer service for users of the Calling Card. PrimeCall will ensure the outsourcing of customer service. DeltaThree shall have no say in the outsourced costumer services and PrimeCall will have the last decision making. DeltaThree shall reimburse PrimeCall for all customer service expenses directly related to users of the Calling Card. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the level of customer service will be determined solely by PrimeCall.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that while PrimeCall negotiates and contracts customer service, the 'level of customer service' is subject to DeltaThree's approval. The modified text removes DeltaThree's approval power and gives PrimeCall full control over customer service standards. This creates a contradiction concerning who ultimately decides the quality and standards of customer service, and creates an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.05 [Customer Service]. Customer Service. PrimeCall shall negotiate and contract on behalf of DeltaThree to out-source customer service for users of the Calling Card. PrimeCall will ensure the outsourcing of customer service. DeltaThree shall have no say in the outsourced costumer services and PrimeCall will have the last decision making. DeltaThree shall reimburse PrimeCall for all customer service expenses directly related to users of the Calling Card. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the level of customer service will be determined solely by PrimeCall.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants PrimeCall sole decision-making power over the level of customer service, directly contradicting the original text where DeltaThree had approval power over the same aspect. This creates a clear in-text contradiction (Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction), as the two sections define the control over customer service standards differently, leading to potential disputes and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any third-party without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states, that PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree. By inserting 'PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any third-party without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.' creates an in text contradiction, because it means that even if is not any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business, it can assign this agreement to anyone.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9.09 [Assignment]. Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any third-party without the prior written consent of DeltaThree. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies conditions under which PrimeCall can assign the agreement, requiring DeltaThree's consent for assignments outside of affiliates, mergers, consolidations, or sales. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating 'PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any third-party without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.' This creates a clear inconsistency with the initial restriction, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability, as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted text includes the original restrictions of PrimeCall's assignment rights.
